attacking_style: Does not initiate decisive attacks but responds to accelerations from stronger climbers, typically staying with moves until final 3-4km on summit finishes before being distanced.
team_tactics: Israel Premier Tech shows inconsistent coordination; teammates animate early but lack cohesive positioning when key moves happen, forcing Frigo to chase or respond individually rather than launching from organized support.
terrain_preference: Mountain stages where resilience and recovery between efforts matter more than explosive power; can survive multiple selections and regain contact after being dropped.
vulnerability: Final 3-4km on sustained climbs when stronger pure climbers accelerate; lacks finishing kick to convert disadvantaged positions into wins even when recovering well.
key_patterns: Dropped and regains contact multiple times per stage, showing exceptional recovery ability (2 races)
Matches stronger climbers deep into climbs but fades inside final 4km (2 observations)
Team lacks tactical cohesion when decisive moves occur, forcing individual chase efforts (2 instances)
Competitive in sprint finishes from compromised positions but cannot quite convert (1 near-win)
form_trajectory: positive: improving stage race durability through Giro, matching stronger climbers deeper into climbs with better resilience
